title: Badgers Colts have mixed success against Western Province
description: GEORGE NEWS - The Six Gun Grill Garden Route...
bodyText:
GEORGE NEWS - The Six Gun Grill Garden Route Badgers Colts team had mixed success last weekend when they took on their Western Province counterparts in Cape Town.

In the three-day match the Badgers managed to finish with a credible draw, but in the limited over match the Oudtshoorn-based team suffered an 85-run defeat.

In the three-day match the Badgers, who batted first, posted 313 in their first innings, with Keenan Vieira (105) and Pheko Moletsane (119) scoring centuries. Western Province responded with 366/4 thanks to impressive batting performances by Christiaan Oberholzer (157), Josh Chippendale (85) and Josh Breed (65).

With a deficit of 53 runs on the first innings, it was again Vieira with an innings of 83 who played a pivotal role with the bat to enable the Badgers to post 266 (all out). He received good support from Ruan Terblanche (49), Hayden Sweet (37) and Heath Richards (34).


> Being set a target of 214, Western Province had their backs against the wall at 96/7 at stumps on the third and final day with the match ending in a draw.


In the one day match that was played at Newlands, Western Province batted first and posted a competitive 302/8 in their 50 overs. The Badgers, despite a solid start thanks to Mark Andrianatos (82) and Breyten Plaatjies (46), was dismissed for 217 for a defeat of 85 runs. Spin bowler Sinalo Gobeni, who was impressive with the ball in the three day match, bagged 6/41.

The summarised score cards are:

Three day match

Garden Route Badgers 1st innings 313 all out (Keenan Vieira 105, Pheko Moletsane 119, Heath Richards 28, Maheer Eksteen 4/45, Sinalo Gobeni 4/30) and 2nd innings 266 all out (Ruan Terblanche 49, Hayden Sweet 37, Vieira 83, Richards 34)

Western Province 366/4 declared (Josh Chippendale 85, Christiaan Oberholzer 157, Josh Breed 65) and 2nd innings 96/7 (Chippendale 36, Breed 30, Hlompo Modimokoane 2/30, Enathi Kitshini 3/27)

Match drawn

One day match

Western Province 302/8 (Josh Chippendale 56, Ismaeel Prince 34, Kai Curren 43, Sinalo Gobeni 50, Jordan Wolf 49, Reagon Rhode 2/44)

Garden Route Badgers 217 all out (Mark Andrianatos 82, Breyton Plaatjies 46, Heath Richards 31, Sinalo Gobeni 6/41)

Western Province won by 85 runs

‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’
